name: Arr
comment:
An `Arr` describes an array where you know more about the amount of elements.

    Array.empty |> Array.get 0
    --> Nothing

    Arr.empty |> Arr.at nat0 FirstToLast
    --> compile-time error

Is this any useful? Let's look at an example:

> You have an array of 1+ elements. Get us the greatest value.

    withArray : Array comparable -> Maybe comparable
    withArray array =
        array
            |> Array.foldl
                (\a mayB ->
                    case maybeC of
                        Just b ->
                            max a b

                        Nothing ->
                            a
                )
                Nothing

    withArr : Arr (In (Nat1Plus minMinus1_) max_) comparable -> comparable
    withArr =
        Arr.foldWith FirstToLast max

The `Array` type doesn't give us the info that it contains 1+ elements. `Arr` simply knows more about the length at compile time, so you can e.g. call `foldWith` without a worry.

@docs Arr


# create

@docs fromArray, fromList, repeat, nats, minNats, random


## exact

@docs empty, from1

[Skip to `from16`](Arr#from16).

@docs from2, from3, from4, from5, from6, from7, from8, from9, from10, from11, from12, from13, from14, from15, from16


# scan

@docs length, at


# modify

@docs replaceAt, updateAt, reverse, resize


## filter

@docs when, dropWhen, whenJust


## part

@docs take, takeMax, groupsOf


# transform

@docs map, fold, foldWith, toArray, toList, serialize


## combine

@docs map2, map3, map4


## drop information

@docs lowerMinLength


## restore information

@docs restoreMaxLength

aliases.name: Arr
aliases.comment:
An `Arr` describes an array where you know more about the amount of elements.


## types

  - amount >= 5

```
Arr (Min Nat5) ...
```

  - 2 <= amount <= 12

```
Arr (In Nat2 (Nat12Plus a_)) ...
```


### as argument types

  - = 15

```
Arr (Only Nat15) ...
```

  - amount >= 4

```
Arr (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4_) max_) ...
```

  - 4 <= amount <= 15

```
Arr (In (Nat4Plus minMinus4_) Nat15) ...
```


## as storage types

To store in your `Model` for example (which means: no any type variables)

  - = 15

```
Arr (Only Nat15) ...
```

  - amount >= 4

```
Arr (Min Nat4) ...
```

  - 4 <= amount <= 15

```
Arr (In Nat4 Nat15) ...
```
aliases.type: Typed.Typed Typed.Checked Internal.ArrTag Typed.Internal (Internal.Content length element)

values.name: at
values.comment:
The element at a valid position in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from4 1 2 3 4
        |> Arr.at nat1 FirstToLast
    --> 2

    Arr.from4 1 2 3 4
        |> Arr.at nat1 LastToFirst
    --> 3
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) max_) element -> element

values.name: dropWhen
values.comment:
Remove values when a condition is met.

    Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5
        |> Arr.dropWhen (\n -> n < 3)
    --> Arr.from3 3 4 5
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat5Plus a_)) number_
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min_ max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 max) element

values.name: empty
values.comment:
No elements.

    Arr.empty
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 atLeast0) element
        |> InArr.push ":)"
    --> : Arr (In Nat1 (Nat1Plus atLeast0)) String
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 atLeast0_) element_

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Reduce an `Arr` in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/indique/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.fold FirstToLast (++) ""
    --> "live"

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.fold LastToFirst (++) ""
    --> "evil"
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> result -> result) -> result -> Arr.Arr length_ element -> result

values.name: foldWith
values.comment:
A fold in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) where the initial result is the first value in the `Arr`.

    Arr.foldWith FirstToLast maximum
        (Arr.from3 234 345 543)
    --> 543

    Arr.foldWith LastToFirst (++)
        (Arr.from3 "m" "l" "e")
    --> "elm"

Often, calling `map` before `foldWith` is helpful.

    Arr.map String.fromInt
        >> Arr.foldWith FirstToLast (++)
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> element -> element) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1_) max_) element -> element

values.name: from1
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 1 element.
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat1 (TypeNats.Nat1Plus a_)) element

values.name: from10
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 10 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat10 (TypeNats.Nat10Plus a_)) element

values.name: from11
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 11 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat11 (TypeNats.Nat11Plus a_)) element

values.name: from12
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 12 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat12 (TypeNats.Nat12Plus a_)) element

values.name: from13
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 13 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat13 (TypeNats.Nat13Plus a_)) element

values.name: from14
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 14 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat14 (TypeNats.Nat14Plus a_)) element

values.name: from15
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 15 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat15 (TypeNats.Nat15Plus a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 16 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat16 (TypeNats.Nat16Plus a_)) element

values.name: from2
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 2 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat2 (TypeNats.Nat2Plus a_)) element

values.name: from3
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 3 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat3 (TypeNats.Nat3Plus a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 4 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat4 (TypeNats.Nat4Plus a_)) element

values.name: from5
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 5 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat5 (TypeNats.Nat5Plus a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 6 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat6 (TypeNats.Nat6Plus a_)) element

values.name: from7
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 7 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat7 (TypeNats.Nat7Plus a_)) element

values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 8 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat8 (TypeNats.Nat8Plus a_)) element

values.name: from9
values.comment: Create an `Arr` with exactly 9 elements in this order.
values.type: element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat9 (TypeNats.Nat9Plus a_)) element

values.name: fromArray
values.comment:
Create an `Arr` from an `Array`. As every `Array` has `>= 0` elements:

    Arr.fromArray arrayFromSomewhere
    --> : Arr (Min Nat0)

Don't use it this way:

    Arr.fromArray
        (Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ])
    --> big no

Tell the compiler if you know the amount of elements. Make sure the it knows as much as you!

    Arr.from7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6
    --> ok

    Arr.nats nat7
    --> big yes
values.type: Array.Array element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min TypeNats.Nat0) element

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Short for `Array.fromList list |> Arr.fromArray`. Create an `Arr` from a `List`. As every `List` has `>= 0` elements:

    Arr.fromList listFromSomewhere
    --> : Arr (Min Nat0)

Don't use it this way:

    Arr.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ]
    --> big no!

Tell the compiler if you know the amount of elements. Make sure the it knows as much as you!

    Arr.from7 0 1 2 3 4 5 6
    --> ok

    Arr.nats nat7
    --> big yes
values.type: List.List element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min TypeNats.Nat0) element

values.name: groupsOf
values.comment:
Split the `Arr` into equal-sized chunks in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    { groups : the Arr divided into equal-sized Arrs
    , less : values to one side which aren't enough
    }

    Arr.from7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.groupsOf nat5 FirstToLast
    --> { groups =
    -->     Arr.from1 (Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5)
    -->     : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a_))
    -->         (Arr (In Nat5 (Nat5Plus b_)) number_)
    --> , remaining =
    -->     Arr.from2 6 7
    -->     : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat5Plus c_)) number_
    --> }

    Arr.from7 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.groupsOf nat5 LastToFirst
    --> { groups = Arr.from1 (Arr.from5 3 4 5 6 7) : ...
    --> , remaining = Arr.from2 1 2 : ...
    --> }
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minGroupSizeMinus1) maxGroupSize groupSizeIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min_ max) element -> { groups : Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 max) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minGroupSizeMinus1) maxGroupSize) element), remaining : Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 maxGroupSize) element }

values.name: length
values.comment:
The amount of elements.

    Arr.length (Arr.from3 1 2 3)
    --> nat3 : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat3Plus a_))

    Arr.length between3And5Elements
    --> : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat5Plus a_))

    Arr.length atLeast3Elements
    --> : Nat (Min Nat3)
values.type: Arr.Arr length element_ -> Nat.Nat length

values.name: lowerMinLength
values.comment:
Use a lower minimum length in the type.

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
    ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `Arr (Min Nat3) ...`

    [ atLeast3Elements
    , atLeast4Elements
        |> Arr.lowerMinLength nat3
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn newMin min lowerIfN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In newMin max) element

values.name: map
values.comment:
Change every element.

    aToZ : Arr (In Nat26 (Nat26Plus a_)) Char
    aToZ =
        Arr.nats nat26
            |> Arr.map (val >> inABC)

    inABC index =
        ('a' |> Char.toCode)
            + index
            |> Char.fromCode

`val` refers to [`Typed.val`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typed-value/latest/Typed#val).
values.type: (element -> mappedElement) -> Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length mappedElement

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Combine the elements of 2 `Arr`s into new elements.
If one list is longer, its extra elements are not used.

    teamLifes aBoard bBoard =
        Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lifes + b.lifes)
            aBoard
            bBoard

The length of all `Arr`s must be in the same range.

  - If 1 `Arr`'s maximum length isn't known:

```
Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lifes + b.lifes)
    (aBoard |> MinArr.value)
    (bBoard |> MinArr.value)

aBoard : Arr (In Nat2 someMax) Field
bBoard : Arr (In Nat2 otherMax) Field
```

  - If 1 `Arr` has a higher minimum length:

```
Arr.map2 (\a b -> a.lifes + b.lifes)
    (aBoard |> Arr.lowerMinLength nat2)
    bBoard

aBoard : Arr (In Nat5 Nat10) Field
```
values.type: (a -> b -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: map3
values.comment: Combine the elements of 3 `Arr`s into new elements. Works like [map2](Arr#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length c -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: map4
values.comment: Combine the elements of 4 `Arr`s into new elements. Works like [map2](Arr#map2).
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> combinedElement) -> Arr.Arr length a -> Arr.Arr length b -> Arr.Arr length c -> Arr.Arr length d -> Arr.Arr length combinedElement

values.name: minNats
values.comment:
Increasing natural numbers. In the end, there are `length` numbers. Use [nats](Arr#nats) if you know the maximum length.

    Arr.nats nat5

    Arr.nats between2And10

If not:

    Arr.minNats atLeast10
    --> : Arr (Min Nat10)
    -->     (Nat (Min Nat10))

    from first length =
        Arr.minNats length
            |> Arr.map (MinNat.add first)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLength maxLength ifN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 maxLength))

values.name: nats
values.comment:
Increasing natural numbers. In the end, there are `length` numbers.

    Arr.nats between2And10
    --> : Arr
    -->     (In Nat2 (Nat10Plus a))
    -->     (Nat (In Nat0 (Nat9Plus a)))

    from first length =
        Arr.nats length
            |> Arr.map (InNat.add first)

If you want to use a `Nat` as the length, but you dont know the maximum, e.g.

    Arr.nats (Nat.intAtLeast nat5 someInt) -- error

use [minNats](Arr#minNats).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLength (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxLengthMinus1) lengthIfN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxLengthMinus1)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 maxLengthMinus1))

values.name: random
values.comment:
Generate a given amount of elements and put them in an `Arr`.

    Arr.random nat5 (Random.float 0 1)
    --> : Random.Generator (Arr (In Nat5 (Nat5Plus a_)) Float)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Random.Generator element -> Random.Generator (Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element)

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
An `Arr` with a given amount of same elements.

    Arr.repeat nat4 'L'
    --> Arr.from4 'L' 'L' 'L' 'L'
    --> : Arr (In Nat4 (Nat4Plus a)) Char


    Arr.repeat atLeast3 'L'
    --> : Arr (Min Nat3) Char
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element

values.name: replaceAt
values.comment:
Set the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat2 FirstToLast "confusion"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "am" "confusion"

    Arr.from3 "I" "am" "ok"
        |> Arr.replaceAt nat1 LastToFirst "feel"
    --> Arr.from3 "I" "feel" "ok"
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) max) element

values.name: resize
values.comment:
Resize an `Arr` in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/), padding with a given value.

    Arr.resize LastToFirst nat4 0
        (Arr.from2 1 2)
    --> Array.from4 0 0 1 2

    Arr.resize LastToFirst nat2 0
        (Array.from3 1 2 3)
    --> Array.from2 2 3

    Arr.resize FirstToLast nat4 0
        (Array.from2 1 2)
    --> Array.from4 1 2 0 0

    Arr.resize FirstToLast nat2 0
        (Array.from3 1 2 3)
    --> Array.from2 1 2

This is a quick way to gain some type-level knowledge about the length.
values.type: LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn newMin newMax ifN_) -> element -> Arr.Arr length_ element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In newMin newMax) element

values.name: restoreMaxLength
values.comment:
Make an `Arr` with a fixed maximum length fit into functions with require a higher maximum length.

While designing argument annotations as general as possible:

    atMost18Elements : Arr (In min Nat18) ...

The argument in `atMost18Elements` should also fit in `atMost19Elements`.

    atMost19Elements theArgument
    --> error :(

    atMost19Elements
        (theArgument |> Arr.restoreMaxLength nat18)
    --> works
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn max newMax ifN_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min newMax) element

values.name: reverse
values.comment:
Flip the order of the elements.

    Arr.from5 "l" "i" "v" "e"
        |> Arr.reverse
    --> Arr.from5 "e" "v" "i" "l"
values.type: Arr.Arr length element -> Arr.Arr length element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s with a specific amount of elements.

    import Serialize


    -- we can't start if we have no worlds to choose from!
    serializeGameRow :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Arr (Only Nat10) GameField)
    serializeGameRow =
        Arr.serialize nat10 serializeGameField

    encode : Arr (Only Nat10) GameField -> Bytes
    encode =
        Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeGameRow

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (Only Nat10) GameField)
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeGameRow
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Serialize.Codec String.String element -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element)

values.name: take
values.comment:
A certain number of elements from a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.take nat7 FirstToLast
    --> : Arr (In Nat3 Nat7) ...

Use [`takeMax`](Arr#takeMax) if you don't know the exact amount of elements to take.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N taken atLeastTaken (Nat.Is takenToMin_ Nat.To min) is_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In taken atLeastTaken) element

values.name: takeMax
values.comment:
A certain number of elements from a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from8 0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7
        |> Arr.takeMax nat7 between3And7 FirstToLast
    --> : Arr (In Nat3 Nat7) ...

The first number is the maximum taken amount. The second number is the amount of taken elements.

Use [`take`](Arr#take) if you know the exact amount of elements to take.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N maxTaken atLeastMaxTaken (Nat.Is maxTakenToMin_ Nat.To min) is_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minTaken maxTaken takenIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken atLeastMaxTaken) element

values.name: toArray
values.comment:
Convert the `Arr` to an `Array`.
Just do this in the end.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can.

    Arr.nats nat5
        |> Arr.map val
        |> Arr.toArray
    --> Array.fromList [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: Arr.Arr length_ element -> Array.Array element

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Short for `Arr.toArray arr |> Array.toList`. Convert the `Arr` to a `List`.
Just do this in the end.
Try to keep extra information as long as you can.

    Arr.nats nat5
        |> Arr.map val
        |> Arr.toList
    --> [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
values.type: Arr.Arr length_ element -> List.List element

values.name: updateAt
values.comment:
Change the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/) based on its previous value.

    Arr.from3 1 20 30
        |> Arr.updateAt nat0 FirstToLast ((*) 10)
    --> Arr.from3 10 20 30

    Arr.from3 10 20 -30
        |> Arr.updateAt nat0 LastToFirst (\x -> -x)
    --> Arr.from3 10 20 30
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> (element -> element) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) max) element

values.name: when
values.comment:
Only keep values that satisfy a test.

    Arr.from5 1 2 3 4 5
        |> Arr.when (\n -> n >= 3)
    --> Arr.from3 3 4 5
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat5Plus a_)) number_
values.type: (element -> Basics.Bool) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 max) element

values.name: whenJust
values.comment:
Take every `Just value` & drop every `Nothing`.

    Arr.from3 (Just "This") Nothing (Just "fine")
        |> Arr.whenJust
    --> Arr.from2 "This" "fine"
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a_)) String

Often, calling `map` before `values` is helpful. Many call this combination "filterMap".

    Arr.from3 "1.2" "2" "hello"
        |> Arr.map String.toInt
        |> Arr.whenJust
    --> Arr.from1 2
    --> : Arr (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a_)) Int
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In min_ max) (Maybe.Maybe value) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In TypeNats.Nat0 max) value

name: InArr

values.name: append
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `Arr` with an exact amount of elements to the right.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.append nat3 (Arr.from3 4 5 6)
    --> Arr.from6 1 2 3 4 5 6
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) (Nat.Is max Nat.To sumMax)) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Only added) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In sumMin sumMax) element

values.name: appendIn
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `Arr` which has multiple possible amounts to the right.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.appendIn nat3 nat5
            arrWith3To5Elements
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 (Nat8Plus a))

Use [`append`](InArr#append) if the appended `Arr` has an exact amount of elements.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMin atLeastAddedMin_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To appendedMin) addedMinIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMax atLeastAddedMax_ (Nat.Is max Nat.To appendedMax) addedMaxIs_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In addedMin addedMax) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In appendedMin appendedMax) element

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Elements after a certain number of elements in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from4 0 1 2 3
        |> InArr.drop nat2 LastToFirst
    --> Arr.from2 0 1

    between6And10Elements
        |> InArr.drop nat2 FirstToLast
    --> : Arr (In Nat4 (Nat10Plus a_)) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N dropped_ atLeastDropped_ (Nat.Is minTaken Nat.To min) (Nat.Is maxTaken Nat.To max)) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken maxTaken) element

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Put an element in the `Arr` at a given index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    Arr.from3 'a' 'c' 'd'
        |> InArr.insertAt nat1 FirstToLast 'b'
    --> Arr.from4 'a' 'b' 'c' 'd'

    Arr.from3 'a' 'c' 'd'
        |> Array.insertAt nat2 LastToFirst 'b'
    --> Array.from4 'a' 'b' 'c' 'd'
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat2Plus minMinus1) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus max)) element

values.name: isLength
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length. Is it `LessOrEqualOrGreater`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    chooseFormation :
        Arr (In min Nat50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        case
            characters
                |> InArr.isLength nat7 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.Equal only7 ->
                SpecialAttack only7

            Nat.Less atMost6 ->
                Retreat atMost6

            Nat.Greater atLeast8 ->
                Fight atLeast8
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N (TypeNats.Nat1Plus valueMinus1) atLeastValue (Nat.Is a_ Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastValueMinus1)) (Nat.Is valueToMax_ Nat.To max)) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToValue_ Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus valueMinus1))) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Nat.LessOrEqualOrGreater (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastValueMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus valueMinus1) atLeastValue) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat2Plus valueMinus1) max) element)

values.name: isLengthAtLeast
values.comment:
Is its length `BelowOrAtLeast` as big as a `Nat`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    first5 :
        Arr (In min max) element
        -> Maybe (Arr (In Nat5 max) element)
    first5 arr =
        case
            arr |> MinArr.isLengthAtLeast nat5 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.Below _ ->
                Nothing

            Nat.EqualOrGreater atLeast5 ->
                Just atLeast5
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowerBound (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) (Nat.Is atLeastRange_ Nat.To max) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest (Nat.Is lowestToMin Nat.To min) (Nat.Is (TypeNats.Nat1Plus lowestToLowerBound_) Nat.To lowerBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Nat.BelowOrAtLeast (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerBound max) element)

values.name: isLengthAtMost
values.comment:
Is its length `AtMostOrAbove` a given length?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    -- at least 3 and only up to 50 tags
    tag :
        Arr (In (Nat3Plus orHigherMin) Nat50) String
        -> a
        -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags :
        Arr (In (Nat3Plus orHigherMin) max)
        -> a
        -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        case
            array
                |> Arr.fromArray
                |> InArr.isLengthAtMost nat50 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.EqualOrLess atMost53 ->
                tag value atMost53 |> Just

            Nat.Above _ ->
                Nothing
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N upperBound atLeastUpperBound (Nat.Is (TypeNats.Nat1Plus greaterRange_) Nat.To max) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToUpperBound_ Nat.To upperBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Nat.AtMostOrAbove (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastUpperBound) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus upperBound) max) element)

values.name: isLengthInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is its length `BelowOrInOrAboveRange`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    chooseFormation :
        Arr (In min Nat50) Character
        -> Formation
    chooseFormation characters =
        case
            characters
                |> InArr.isLengthInRange nat10 nat20 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.InRange between10And20 ->
                SpecialAttack between10And20

            Nat.BelowRange atMost9 ->
                Retreat atMost9

            Nat.AboveRange atLeast21 ->
                Fight atLeast21
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowerBound (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) (Nat.Is lowerBoundToUpperBound_ Nat.To upperBound) lowerBoundIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N upperBound atLeastUpperBound (Nat.Is upperBoundToMax_ Nat.To max) upperBoundIs_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToLowerBound_ Nat.To lowerBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Nat.BelowOrInOrAboveRange (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowerBound atLeastUpperBound) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus upperBound) max) element)

values.name: prepend
values.comment:
Add elements of an `Arr` with an exact amount of elements to the left.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.prepend nat3 (Arr.from3 4 5 6)
    --> Arr.from6 4 5 6 1 2 3
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) (Nat.Is max Nat.To sumMax)) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Only added) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In sumMin sumMax) element

values.name: prependIn
values.comment:
Add elements of an `Arr` which has multiple possible amounts to the left.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> InArr.prependIn nat3 nat5
            arrWith3To5Elements
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 (Nat8Plus a))

Use [`prepend`](InArr#prepend) if the appended `Arr` has an exact amount of elements.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMin atLeastAddedMin_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To appendedMin) addedMinIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N addedMax atLeastAddedMax_ (Nat.Is max Nat.To appendedMax) addedMaxIs_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In addedMin addedMax) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In appendedMin appendedMax) element

values.name: push
values.comment:
Put a new element after the others.

    arrWith5To10Elements
        |> InArr.push "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (In Nat6 (Nat11Plus a_)) String
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus min) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus max)) element

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Kick an element out of the `Arr` at a given index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    removeLast arrWith1To10Elements =
        arrWith1To10Elements
            |> InArr.removeAt nat0 LastToFirst
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minMinus1) (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxMinus1)) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minMinus1 maxMinus1) element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s within a minimum & maximum length.

    import Serialize

    serialize10To15Ints :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Arr (In Nat10 (Nat15Plus a_)) Int)
    serialize10To15Ints =
        InArr.serialize Serialize.int nat10 nat15

    encode :
        Arr (In (Nat10Plus orHigherMin_) Nat15) Int
        -> Bytes
    encode =
        Arr.lowerMinLength nat10
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serialize10To15Ints

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (In Nat10 (Nat15Plus a_) Int))
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serialize10To15Ints

For decoded `Arr`s with a length outside of the expected bounds, the `Result` is an error message.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound minUpperBound lowerBoundIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minUpperBound maxUpperBound upperBoundIfN_) -> Serialize.Codec String.String element -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLowerBound maxUpperBound) element)

name: MinArr

values.name: append
values.comment:
Attach elements of an `Arr` to the right.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> MinArr.append nat3 arrWithAtLeast3Elements
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N minAdded atLeastMinAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) is_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minAdded maxAdded_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min sumMin) element

values.name: drop
values.comment:
Elements after a certain number of elements in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    withAtLeast6Elements
        |> MinArr.drop nat2 LastToFirst
    --> : Arr (Min Nat4) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N dropped_ atLeastDropped_ (Nat.Is minTaken Nat.To min) is_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minTaken max) element

values.name: insertAt
values.comment:
Put a new element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    atLeast5Elements
        |> MinArr.insertAt nat0 LastToFirst
            "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) String
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat2Plus minLengthMinus1)) element

values.name: isLength
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length. Is it `LessOrEqualOrGreater`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    convertUserArguments :
        String
        -> Result String (Arr (Only Nat3))
    convertUserArguments =
        let
            wordArr =
                String.words arguments
                    |> Arr.fromList
        in
        case
            wordArr
                |> MinArr.isLength nat3 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.Equal only3 ->
                Ok only3

            Nat.Less atMost2 ->
                Err
                    ("I need 3 arguments, but there are only "
                        ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length atMost2))
                        ++ "."
                    )

            Nat.Greater atLeast4 ->
                Err
                    ("I need exact 3 arguments, so "
                        ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length atLeast4))
                        ++ " is too much."
                    )
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N (TypeNats.Nat1Plus valueMinus1) atLeastValue (Nat.Is a_ Nat.To (TypeNats.Nat1Plus atLeastValueMinus1)) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToValueMinus1_ Nat.To valueMinus1)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Nat.LessOrEqualOrGreater (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest atLeastValueMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus valueMinus1) atLeastValue) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat2Plus valueMinus1)) element)

values.name: isLengthAtLeast
values.comment:
Compare its length to a given exact length.
Is it `BelowOrAtLeast` that value?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    convertUserArguments : String -> Result String (Arr (Min Nat3))
    convertUserArguments arguments =
        let
            wordArr =
                String.words arguments
                    |> Arr.fromList
        in
        case
            wordArr
                |> MinArr.isLengthAtLeast nat3 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.EqualOrGreater atLeast3 ->
                Ok atLeast3

            Nat.Below atMost2 ->
                Err
                    ("I need at least 3 arguments, but there are only "
                        ++ String.fromInt (val (Arr.length atMost2))
                        ++ "."
                    )
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound (TypeNats.Nat1Plus maxLowerBoundMinus1) ifN_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is lowestToMinLowerBound_ Nat.To minLowerBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Nat.BelowOrAtLeast (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest maxLowerBoundMinus1) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min minLowerBound) element)

values.name: isLengthAtMost
values.comment:
Is its length `AtMostOrAbove` a given length?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum length.

    -- only up to 50 tags
    tag : Arr (In min Nat50) String -> a -> Tagged a

    tagIfValidTags : Array String -> a -> Maybe (Tagged a)
    tagIfValidTags array value =
        case
            array
                |> Arr.fromArray
                |> MinArr.isLengthAtMost nat50 { lowest = nat0 }
        of
            Nat.EqualOrLess atMost50 ->
                tag value atMost50 |> Just

            Nat.Above _ ->
                Nothing
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minUpperBound maxUpperBound ifN_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is lowestToMinUpperBound_ Nat.To minUpperBound)) } -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Nat.AtMostOrAbove (Arr.Arr (Nat.In lowest maxUpperBound) element) (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minUpperBound)) element)

values.name: prepend
values.comment:
Add elements of an `Arr` to the left.

    Arr.from3 1 2 3
        |> MinArr.prepend nat3 arrWithAtLeast3Elements
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) ...
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N minAdded atLeastMinAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) is_) -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minAdded maxAdded_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min sumMin) element

values.name: push
values.comment:
Put a new element after the others.

    atLeast5Elements
        |> MinArr.push "becomes the last"
    --> : Arr (Min Nat6) String
values.type: element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLength maxLength_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLength)) element

values.name: removeAt
values.comment:
Kick out the element at an index in a [direction](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-linear-direction/latest/).

    removeLast arrWithAtLeast1Element =
        arrWithAtLeast1Element
            |> MinArr.removeAt nat0 LastToFirst
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn indexMin_ minLengthMinus1 indexIfN_) -> LinearDirection.LinearDirection -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In (TypeNats.Nat1Plus minLengthMinus1) maxLength) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.In minLengthMinus1 maxLength) element

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Arr`s with a minimum amount of elements.

    import Serialize


    -- we can't start if we have no worlds to choose from!
    serializeSaves : Serialize.Codec String (Arr (Min Nat1) World)
    serializeSaves =
        MinArr.serialize nat1 serializeWorld

    encode : Arr (In (Nat1Plus minMinus1_) max_) World -> Bytes
    encode =
        MinArr.value
            >> Arr.lowerMinLength nat1
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeSaves

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Arr (Min Nat1) World)
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeSaves
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ ifN_) -> Serialize.Codec String.String element -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Arr.Arr (Nat.Min min) element)

values.name: value
values.comment:
Convert the `Arr (In min ...)` to a `Arr (Min min)`.

    between4And10Elements |> MinArr.value
    --> : Arr (Min Nat4) ...

There is only 1 situation you should use this.

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1Element, between1And10Elements ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are
> `Arr (Min Nat1) ...`

    [ atLeast1Element
    , between1And10Elements |> MinArr.value
    ]
values.type: Arr.Arr (Nat.In min max_) element -> Arr.Arr (Nat.Min min) element
